mnemonics: Twisted Pair Fights Noise, Coaxial Carries Cable, Fiber Optics Flies Fast
keyPoints: Twisted Pair Cable uses copper wires twisted together to cancel out electromagnetic interference (EMI).
Coaxial Cable has a central conductor, insulating layer, metallic shield, and outer cover, used for cable TV and broadband internet.
Fiber Optic Cable uses light pulses through glass/plastic fibers for high speed, long distance, and secure data transmission.
commonMistakes: Thinking UTP is suitable for long-distance outdoor runs (it's susceptible to interference and is for indoor use).
Assuming Fiber Optic is always the best choice (it has a higher installation cost despite its advantages).
